heading: Air facility closures
label: 912 Air facility closures

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Beginning on , the Secretary may not close a Coast Guard air facility, except as specified by this section.January 1, 2018

children.label: A
children.text: remaining search and rescue capabilities maintain the safety of the maritime public in the area of the air facility;

children.label: B
children.text: regional or local prevailing weather and marine conditions, including water temperatures or unusual tide and current conditions, do not require continued operation of the air facility; and

children.label: C
children.text: Coast Guard search and rescue standards related to search and response times are met.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Prior to closing an air facility, the Secretary shall provide opportunities for public comment, including the convening of public meetings in communities in the area of responsibility of the air facility with regard to the proposed closure or cessation of operations at the air facility.

children.heading: .—Public meetings
children.label: B .—Public meetings
children.text: Prior to convening a public meeting under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall notify each congressional office representing any portion of the area of responsibility of the air station that is the subject to such public meeting of the schedule and location of such public meeting.

children.label: i
children.text: a discussion of the determination made by the Secretary pursuant to paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: a report summarizing the public comments received by the Secretary under paragraph (3)1So in original. Probably should be followed by “; and”.1

children.label: i
children.text: Each member of the House of Representatives who represents a district in which the air facility is located.

children.label: ii
children.text: Each member of the Senate who represents a State in which the air facility is located.

children.label: iii
children.text: Each member of the House of Representatives who represents a district in which assets of the air facility conduct search and rescue operations.

children.label: iv
children.text: Each member of the Senate who represents a State in which assets of the air facility conduct search and rescue operations.

children.label: v
children.text: The Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives.

children.label: vii
children.text: The Committee on Appropriations of the Senate.

children.label: viii
children.text: The Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate.

children.heading: .—Congressional review
children.label: 5 .—Congressional review
children.text: The Secretary may not close, cease operations, or significantly reduce personnel and use of a Coast Guard air facility for which a written notice is provided under paragraph (4)(A) until a period of 18 months beginning on the date on which such notice is provided has elapsed.

children.heading: .—Operational Flexibility
children.label: b .—Operational Flexibility
children.text: The Secretary may implement any reasonable management efficiencies within the air station and air facility network, such as modifying the operational posture of units or reallocating resources as necessary to ensure the safety of the maritime public nationwide.
